Assets.pageTitle: Assets to cover family expenses after death

FutureIncome.pageTitle: Your family's estimated future monthly income, in today's dollars

FutureExpenses.pageTitle: Your family's estimated future monthly expenses, in today's dollars

Expenses.fieldLabel: Total amount necessary today to cover expenses

NowAssets.fieldLabel: Assets you own today which you may convert to cash

FutAssets.fieldLabel: Amount of expenses your family will be able to cover

labels.title: How much life insurance do I need?
labels.answer1: You'll need %InsNeeded% in life insurance to cover expenses.
labels.answer2: You do not need to buy any life insurance to cover expenses.
labels.FutureIncome_row2: Social Security survivor benefits (children at home)
labels.FutureExpenses_row5: Retraining or education expenses for spouse

probate.title: Probate
probate.definition: A legal procedure settled by a state court of law that identifies the heirs to your estate and determines their legally entitled share.

collegesavingsplan.title: College savings plan
collegesavingsplan.definition: A special tax-advantaged plan, operated by a state government or an educational institution, used to save for the college education of a child, grandchild, or other dependent.

estatetaxes.title: Estate taxes
estatetaxes.definition: Taxes that are levied on the assets of an estate. For 2025, the maximum amount of income that is excluded from estate taxes is $13.99 million. The maximum estate and gift tax rate in 2025 is 40%.

inflationrate.title: Inflation rate
inflationrate.definition: A general increase in prices that you pay for goods and services, stated as a yearly rate. A common measure of inflation in the U.S. is the Consumer Price Index (CPI).

taxrates.title: Tax rates
taxrates.definition: The percentage of your taxable income that is owed to the state and federal governments. The tax rate increases as the taxable base amount increases.

savingsinterestrate.title: Savings interest rate
savingsinterestrate.definition: The yearly interest rate you earn on your savings.

survivorsinsurance.title: Survivors insurance
survivorsinsurance.definition: Social Security survivors insurance pays an additional benefit, called a survivors benefit, to the surviving spouse or dependent of a deceased person who was eligible to receive or was receiving Social Security retirement benefits.

inflation.title: Inflation
inflation.definition: A general increase in prices that you pay for goods and services, stated as a yearly rate.

help.textLabel: This tool estimates how much life insurance coverage your family may need to cover future expenses in the event of your untimely death.
A life insurance policy is used to supplement income that your family may earn from:
Nonrecurring events, such as the sale of assets to pay outstanding debts or expenses.
Income from Social Security, pensions, investments, or salaries.
Key assumptions of this tool include:
The results shown are after inflation and taxes

Available assets (including home equity, savings, and other investments) are treated as a source of income.  It is assumed that they are sold at the time of unexpected death and therefore become available as a cash resource.

chartTitles.title: Pie chart of the breakdown of current assets, future assets and insurance needed to cover expenses after death.
